english: daily, what is done or is happening every day; common, general, dṅos-grub t'un-moṅs (dngos grub thun mongs) earthly goods, as well as intellectual endowments, considered as common property, but not spiritual gifts; t'un-mín, t'un-moṅs ma yin-pa (thun min, thun mongs ma yin pa) unusual, uncommon, not for every body; *čig-la čig t'un-moṅ čo* (cig la cig thun mong co) take good care to live together in harmony W.; t'un-moṅ-du (thun mong du) or su (su) in common, in company, jointly; t'un-moṅ (thun mong) by itself is also used as adv., = t'un-spyír (thun spyir), in general.

I. vb., c. accus., sometimes c. dat., 1. to get the better of, to be able to cope with, to be a match for (an enemy), to be able to stand or bear (the cold etc.), to be able to do one harm, to get at one, dug-gis ma t'ub-čiṅ (dug gis ma thub cing) as the poison could not do him any harm Dzl.; to be able to quench, extinguish, keep off e.g. fire, hail Glr.; gźan-gyis mi t'ub-pa (gzhan gyis mi thub pa) invincible, not to be overcome; ṅan dgu t'ub-pa (ngan dgu thub pa) to be able to subdue every thing that is bad Lt.; to have under one's command or control, to keep under, e.g. one's own body; to be able to bear, e.g. mis t'ub-par dka (mis thub par dka) (water from a glacier) is not easily borne by man, i.e. does not agree with him Med.; ras rkyaṅ t'ub-pa (ras rkyang thub pa) to be able to bear a simple cotton dress Mil.; lo brgya t'ub-pa (lo brgya thub pa) to live to (the age of) a hundred years, frq. - 2. with a supine or verbal root, to be able, col. the usual word, in B. gen. nús-pa (nus pa); cf. btúb-pa (btub pa). II. sbst. 1. शक्य a mighty one, one having power and authority, śā-kya-t'ub-pa (shA kya thub pa) Buddha; a wise man, a sage, a saint in general, मुनि. - 2. symb. num. for 7.

also t'úm-pa (thum pa) Cs., t'úm-po (thum po) Sch., 1. cover, covering, wrapper, of a book or a parcel; rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa) Sch. to put (a cover round a thing), to wrap up; *śig-pa* (shig pa) C., W., *sáṅ-pa* (sang pa) C. to take off (a covering); t'úm-čan (thum can) having a cover. - 2. a parcel wrapped up (in paper etc.); °bru-taṅ-t'úm bčas (bru tang thum bcas) together with a small parcel of tea.
a large spoon, a ladle; rag-t'úm (rag thum) a brass ladle, zaṅs-t'úm (zangs thum) a copper ladle.

1. Cs. a declivity (?), prob. only adverbially: down; t'ur-lám (thur lam) a down-hill road; t'úr-la (thur la), t'úr-du (thur du) down, downward, gró-ba (gro ba) to go down, núb-pa (nub pa) to sink down; mgo t'úr-du bstán-te (mgo thur du bstan te) head down, head over heels Stg.; *t'í-pi t'úr-la śúb-če* (thi pi thur la shub ce) W. to uncock one's cap. - 2. v. t'úr-mgo (thur mgo), and t'úr-ma (thur ma).

1. the tip of a spoon, t'úr-mgo tsam (thur mgo tsam) as a measure Mil. - 2. also t'ór-mgo (thor mgo) halter, *t'úr-go čúg-če* (thur go cug ce) W. to bridle, to bit (a horse); *t'úr-la tén-če* (thur la ten ce) W. to strive, to struggle against; to rear. - t'ur-tág (thur tag) the rein, t'ur-mda (thur mda) the end of the rein.
W. *t'ur-maṅ* (thur mang), 1. spoon. - 2. Chinese chopsticks. - 3. a pole Dzl. 702, 4. - 4. a whole class of surgical instruments S.g.

1. egg (acc. to Cunningham a Kashmiri word), t'úl-ta-gir (thul ta gir) pancake. - 2. v. °dúl-ba (dul ba), also substantively: t'ul de min (thul de min) besides this way of converting (people) Pth.; t'ul °óg-tu °júg-pa (thul og tu jug pa) Tar. 25, 16 to keep a tight hand over a person, to discipline one; źiṅ-gi t'ul °débs-pa (zhing gi thul debs pa) Ld.-Glr. to clear land for tillage, ni f.

Cs. also t'úl-po (thul po), dress made of the skins of animals, a furred coat or cloak Mil.; lúg-t'ul (lug thul) dress of sheep-skin, rá-t'ul (ra thul) dress of goat-skin, t'úl-lu (thul lu) the common sheep-skin dress; *t'úl-čan* (thul can) W. wide, not fitting close or tight.

1. pf. to °dúl-ba (dul ba), to tame, curb, check, restrain, Mil.: ṅás °dré-rnams t'úl-nas (ngas dre rnams thul nas) the goblins having been subdued by me; las nyon-moṅs t'úl-ba dka (las nyon mongs thul ba dka) it is difficult to check a sinful deed Mil.; participle: tamed, civilized; converted. - 2. to roll or wind up Lh.
Ld. impressive, nearly the same as t'úr-re (thur re).
1. bad = t'ú-ba (thu ba), prov.; 2. v. °t'ú-ba (thu ba).
